name: Mirador del Alto de las Peñas – circular from Terriente
name_original: Mirador del Alto de las Peñas – circular desde Terriente
region_intro: Jogging around Terriente offers a unique opportunity to explore the diverse landscapes of the Sierra de Albarracín. The region is characterized by mountainous terrain, extensive pine forests, and elevated juniper groves, providing varied and scenic routes. Joggers can discover natural features such as springs, waterfalls, and riverine landscapes, with routes often featuring a mix of gentle meadows and more challenging inclines. This varied topography makes for excellent trail running, allowing for changes in cadence and ground contact. Best jogging routes around Terriente The most popular jogging route is Ojos del Cabriel and cascada del Molino de San Pedro – circular by La Herrería Waterfall, a 4.6 miles (7.5 km) trail that takes about 55 minutes to complete, passing by notable natural sites. Another top favourite among local runners is Mirador del Alto de las Peñas – circular from Terriente, a moderate 6.6 miles (10.7 km) path offering panoramic views from the high points. Local runners also love the Fuente del Buey Local Trail, an 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 29 minutes. Jogging around Terriente is defined by extensive pine forests, elevated juniper groves, and picturesque waterfalls, with routes offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Terriente are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 2 reviews. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore Terriente's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Terriente?
A: There are over 9 dedicated running routes around Terriente, offering a variety of experiences for different fitness levels. The majority of these are moderate, with a few more challenging options for experienced runners.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on a run around Terriente?
A: Running around Terriente offers a diverse and striking natural landscape. You'll encounter extensive pine forests, elevated juniper groves, and mountainous terrain. Many routes feature a mix of gentle meadows and more challenging inclines, providing a natural 'fartlek session' with varied ground contact.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Terriente?
A: Yes, several routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983323510/mirador-del-alto-de-las-penas-circular-from-terriente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Alto de las Peñas – circular from Terriente&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate 6.6-mile (10.7 km) path offering panoramic views. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983156660/ojos-del-cabriel-and-cascada-del-molino-de-san-pedro-circular-by-la-herreria-waterfall" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Cabriel and cascada del Molino de San Pedro – circular by La Herrería Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, a 4.6-mile (7.5 km) trail.

Q: Can I find running routes with waterfalls near Terriente?
A: Absolutely! The region is known for its picturesque waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983156660/ojos-del-cabriel-and-cascada-del-molino-de-san-pedro-circular-by-la-herreria-waterfall" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Cabriel and cascada del Molino de San Pedro – circular by La Herrería Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; route passes by notable natural sites including a waterfall. Additionally, the Cascada de la Batida and Cascada del Río de la Fuente del Berro are beautiful natural features in the area that can be incorporated into longer runs or visited as scenic points.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly jogging routes in Terriente?
A: While most routes are classified as moderate, the varied terrain means you can often find sections suitable for beginners. The region's trails offer a mix of gentle meadows, which are great for those new to trail running. For a moderate but accessible option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983156660/ojos-del-cabriel-and-cascada-del-molino-de-san-pedro-circular-by-la-herreria-waterfall" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Cabriel and cascada del Molino de San Pedro – circular by La Herrería Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.6 miles (7.5 km) long.

Q: What are some scenic running trails with good views in Terriente?
A: For breathtaking views, we recommend the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983323510/mirador-del-alto-de-las-penas-circular-from-terriente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Alto de las Peñas – circular from Terriente&lt;/a&gt;. This route offers panoramic vistas from its high points. The entire Sierra de Albarracín natural park provides a stunning backdrop, with many trails winding through extensive pine forests and elevated juniper groves, ensuring a visually appealing run.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Terriente?
A: The running trails in Terriente are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the diverse and striking natural landscapes, the tranquility of the pine forests, and the variety of terrain that makes for an engaging and rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any historical or natural attractions to see along the running routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in both natural and historical attractions. You can explore sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4547706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Shelter of the Archer of the Enclosed Ravines&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3930079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prado del Navazo Bulls Rock Shelter&lt;/a&gt;, which are ancient historical sites. Natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4542067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moricacho of the Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and various gorges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4542097" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco Mingo Marco&lt;/a&gt; offer stunning scenery. The Ojos del Cabriel and Fuente del Buey are also highlighted as natural heritage attractions.

Q: Are there dog-friendly running trails in Terriente?
A: While specific dog-friendly designations for each trail are not always available, the natural park environment around Terriente, with its extensive forests and open spaces, is generally welcoming for dogs. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife, and to respect local regulations.

Q: What is the best season for running in Terriente?
A: Terriente's mountainous terrain and forests make it enjoyable for running across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer mornings and evenings are also ideal to avoid the midday heat. Even winter can be beautiful, with the possibility of crisp, clear runs through the pine and juniper groves.

Q: Are there any longer running trails for more experienced runners?
A: For those looking for a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983315048/fuente-del-buey-local-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Buey Local Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.1-mile (13.0 km) route that leads through varied terrain. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983268958/jabalon-and-ermita-de-san-cristobal-running-from-jabaloyas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jabalon and Ermita de San Cristobal - running from Jabaloyas&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 11.3 km.
tips: Picturesque town in the Sierra de Albarracín region. Its singular distribution of streets and blocks without much unity among themselves, with showy and picturesque houses and squares. In this urbanism, typical of the cattle populations of the mountains, the Church of El Salvador stands out without a doubt, the result of successive interventions carried out throughout the 16th century.
Panoramic and astrological point to appreciate not only the views of the mountains and the Montes Universales del Algarve, but also at night it is an ideal place to see stars.
Site with very nice views of the valley and La Hoya a few km from the town of Terriente. It is a very picturesque place, very green in spring-summer given the crops that are grown there.
